hook: Most landlords miss 5% net yield — here's exactly why.

slides.title: Singapore Rental Reality Check
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 URA data decoded: which districts deliver yield, which destroy it

slides.headline: Sims Urban Oasis (D14) — 2BR Median Rent
slides.change: Gross yield ~4.5% · Net yield ~3.6% after costs
slides.footnote: Median of 20 URA-registered 2BR leases at Sims Drive, 26Q1. Purchase psf assumed at $1,600 for a 650 sqft unit (~$1.04M).

slides.headline: Wallich Residence (D02) — 1BR Rent vs Purchase Cost
slides.change: Gross yield ~3.99% · Net yield ~3.2% — luxury premium kills the math
slides.footnote: Average of Jan–Mar 2026 URA-registered 1BR leases at ~650 sqft. Purchase psf estimated $3,350 (~$2.18M). Yield drops further for 3BR units.

slides.headline: Rosewood Suites (D25) — 2BR Rent PSF
slides.change: Strongest gross yield proxy in dataset at ~5.7% gross · ~4.5% net
slides.footnote: Mar 2026 URA lease: 2BR at ~650 sqft, $3,000/mo. D25 purchase psf ~$950–$1,050. Highest gross yield in this data batch — but net still falls short of 5% after tax, maintenance, vacancy.

slides.headline: The Luxury Yield Trap: D10 vs D14
slides.body: Shangri-La Residences (D10) commands $9,752–$14,943/mo for 1,550–2,050 sqft units. Sounds impressive. But at D10 purchase psf of ~$3,000+, gross yields collapse to 2.5–3.0%. Meanwhile Sims Urban Oasis (D14) 1BR at $3,400/mo on a ~$680K entry price delivers 5.99% gross — nearly double. The market pays you more per dollar invested when you buy mass market, not prestige.

slides.headline: Tanjong Ria (D15) — 3BR Rent Range Q1 2026
slides.footnote: URA 26Q1. 3BR units at 1,200–1,400 sqft. At estimated D15 resale psf ~$1,200, gross yield ~3.8–4.0%. Marine Parade location supports rental demand but pricing has compressed yield.

slides.headline: Why No Project in This Dataset Clears 5% Net
slides.body: True net yield = gross rent minus property tax (~10% AV), maintenance fees ($300–$700/mo), agent commission (½ month/year), vacancy (1 month/year), and income tax. On a $1M condo grossing 4.5%, realistic net lands at 3.4–3.7%. To clear 5% net in Singapore today you need: (1) entry price well below $1,000 psf, (2) rent psf above $5.00/mo, or (3) HDB approved-subletting plays. D25 Rosewood Suites comes closest in this dataset.

slides.headline: Hottest Volume District: D14 (Geylang / Sims)
slides.change: Most URA rental transactions in this data batch — Q1 2026
slides.footnote: Sims Urban Oasis alone recorded 67 URA lease entries across Jan–Mar 2026. Tenant depth here is real. Vacancy risk is structurally lower than low-volume luxury projects.

slides.title: Want Your Property's True Net Yield Calculated?

caption:
Most landlords think they're earning 4–5% yield. The Q1 2026 URA data tells a different story.

Sims Urban Oasis (D14) 2BR leases are clearing a median $3,900/mo — that's a 4.5% gross yield on a ~$1.04M entry. Once you strip out property tax, maintenance, agent fees and vacancy, net yield lands at ~3.6%. Wallich Residence (D02) looks glamorous at $7,250/mo for a 1BR — until you price in the $2.18M purchase cost and watch gross yield collapse to 3.99%. Even the strongest play in this dataset, Rosewood Suites (D25) 2BR at $3,000/mo, only hits ~5.7% gross (~4.5% net). No project in the Q1 2026 URA batch clears 5% net yield on a clean cost model. Knowing this changes where you buy, what size you buy, and what price you accept. DM me for a free yield stress-test on any unit you're eyeing.
